_meta.name: Evidence-Based Frameworks Index
_meta.description: Master index for all evidence-based therapeutic framework libraries
_meta.usage_note: These frameworks provide structural credibility. Apply brand voice, cognitive level and proprietary philosophy on top.

categories.name: Skill Building
categories.description: Frameworks for understanding how people learn and develop new skills
categories.use_when: Designing content that teaches a skill or builds competence over time

categories.name: Behaviour Change
categories.description: Frameworks for understanding and supporting behaviour change
categories.use_when: Content focuses on changing habits, building new behaviours, or understanding why change is hard

categories.name: Goal Setting
categories.description: Frameworks for setting effective goals and creating action plans
categories.use_when: Content includes goal setting exercises or action planning sections

categories.name: Emotional Processing
categories.description: Frameworks for working with difficult emotions
categories.use_when: Content addresses emotional regulation, processing feelings, or understanding emotional reactions

categories.name: Cognitive Work
categories.description: Frameworks for understanding and changing thought patterns
categories.use_when: Content focuses on thoughts, beliefs, cognitive patterns or CBT approaches

categories.name: Relationships
categories.description: Frameworks for understanding and improving relationships
categories.use_when: Content addresses communication, relationship patterns, conflict or connection

categories.name: Recovery
categories.description: Frameworks for maintaining change and managing setbacks
categories.use_when: Content focuses on sustaining progress, preventing relapse, or building ongoing wellness practices

categories.name: Self Discovery
categories.description: Frameworks for understanding yourself and what matters to you
categories.use_when: Content focuses on self understanding, values clarification, or life assessment

categories.name: Trauma Informed
categories.description: Frameworks for understanding and working with trauma
categories.use_when: Content needs to be trauma sensitive, addresses nervous system regulation, or supports trauma recovery

adapting_by_cognitive_level.level_1_academic: Use full technical terminology. Cite sources. Include theoretical nuance.
adapting_by_cognitive_level.level_2_technical_with_support: Use technical terms with explanations. Reference sources.
adapting_by_cognitive_level.level_3_informed: Use accessible language. Focus on practical application. Light attribution.
adapting_by_cognitive_level.level_4_accessible: Use simple language. Focus on the what and how. Skip theoretical background.
adapting_by_cognitive_level.level_5_crisis_accessible: Use only essential information. Very concrete. Immediate application.

usage_guidance.combining_frameworks: Many workbooks use multiple frameworks together. See content_type_recommendations for suggested combinations.
